per_outcome.operationalization: Average score (0-50) from three independent judges using a rubric with five indicators: clarity, evidence credibility, rhetoric, refutation ability, and argument superiority
Binary indicator of whether participant's average judge score exceeded their rival's average score in a debate round
Judge-rated score (0-10) on clarity and validity of the defended position, one dimension of the debate rubric
Judge-rated score (0-10) on credibility of evidence used, one dimension of the debate rubric
Judge-rated score (0-10) on formal quality and rhetoric, one dimension of the debate rubric
Judge-rated score (0-10) on ability to refute rival's arguments, one dimension of the debate rubric
Judge-rated score (0-10) on superiority of arguments used, one dimension of the debate rubric
Self-reported perception of whether preparation time was sufficient, measured via post-experiment survey
Self-reported perception of tool usefulness for debate preparation, measured via post-experiment survey
